nodes.label: AI for code modification vs generation from scratch
nodes.grounding_span: I also avoid having AI create its own code from scratch - I limit it to code modifications

nodes.label: AI as throughput multiplier vs replacement
nodes.grounding_span: I find a way to utilize AI to increase my throughput rather than try to beat it

nodes.label: AI code verbosity and complexity vs human-written clarity
nodes.grounding_span: AI models are very verbose in their code output. The code is far too convoluted for me to understand at first glance

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming partner for approaches vs final decision maker
nodes.grounding_span: AI just helps ensure all approaches are considered. Since it's impossible for it to have complete context... it's rare for us to pick a certain approach

nodes.label: AI task decomposition into small steps vs monolithic tasks
nodes.label_negative: Long conversations with AI on large tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I like to break up tasks and I find it more efficient for token usage as well

nodes.label: Code quality and maintainability
nodes.grounding_span: Writing the code myself ensures I catch any possible errors

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I find a way to utilize AI to increase my throughput

nodes.label: Understanding and learning
nodes.grounding_span: I learn from what the model does and write the code myself

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for modifications but frustration with verbosity
nodes.grounding_span: I can give an instance where I was pretty satisfied with the results but a closer look revealed a lot of flaws

nodes.label: Positive outlook on AI as skill supplement
nodes.grounding_span: I believe that finding more innovative ways to use AI across my work day will supplement my skills rather than replace them

nodes.label: Preference for AI in brainstorming but not final decisions
nodes.grounding_span: AI just helps ensure all approaches are considered

nodes.label: Task decomposition into small verifiable steps
nodes.grounding_span: I like to break up tasks and I find it more efficient for token usage as well

nodes.label: Learning-oriented review of AI output
nodes.grounding_span: I learn from what the model does and write the code myself
